arts.ttl: Arsenal XI vs Newcastle: Confirmed team news, predicted lineup and injury latest for Carabao Cup semi-final
arts.sum: Gunners increasingly short of options in forward areas

arts.ttl: Newcastle United's added transfer complication despite PSR signing as big exit edges closer
arts.sum: Yet, while John Ruddy and Mark Gillespie are training ground leaders who offer vital experience to Eddie Howe's dressing room and Odysseas Vlachodimos could deputise for Nick Pope upon Dubravka leaving, Newcastle are still seeking out potential shot-stoppers for both the short and long-term. Newcastle United are poised to lose Martin Dubravka to Saudi Arabian side Al-Shabab in the coming days, despite the Slovakian currently flourishing as the club's stand-in No 1 between the sticks.

arts.ttl: Depleted Arsenal suffer another injury blow with Ethan Nwaneri sidelined
arts.sum: Nwaneri grabbed the opener in Arsenal’s 1-1 draw at Brighton on Saturday before going off at half-time with a muscle issue.

arts.ttl: Ethan Nwaneri could miss 10 Arsenal matches as Mikel Arteta delivers injury update
arts.sum: Sports Mole looks at the Arsenal games Ethan Nwaneri could miss as Mikel Arteta confirms that the 17-year-old will be sidelined for a few weeks with a muscle injury.

arts.ttl: Mikel Arteta admits something rare just happened in Arsenal training he has not seen before
Alan Shearer in doubt over Ange Postecoglou's Newcastle claim
arts.sum: However, while Newcastle goalscoring legend Alan Shearer admits he's not a fan of the rule, he felt Postecoglou was wrong to bemoan the decision - and pointed out that he failed to mention what Shearer felt was a stonewall penalty for United which was not given. The presumption was that he was furious with big refereeing decisions, with the ball clearly hitting Joelinton's hand before Anthony Gordon's equaliser.

arts.ttl: Arteta: Arsenal 'alert' in market as injuries mount
arts.sum: Mikel Arteta has said that Arsenal are "very alert" in the January transfer market after the London club suffered a fresh injury blow with Ethan Nwaneri out for a "few weeks."

arts.ttl: How Arne Slot sides typically perform in second half of the season as Arsenal sit six points behind Liverpool
Nwaneri out for a few weeks | 'Arsenal in a position to act in window'
arts.sum: Mikel Arteta has confirmed Arsenal midfielder Ethan Nwaneri will be sidelined for a few weeks with a muscular injury.

arts.ttl: Newcastle’s Carabao Cup chance comes at crucial moment in club’s history
arts.sum: Eddie Howe is desperate to end a long trophy drought as decision awaits over potential rebuild of St James’ Park By the time Sunday 16 March dawns, Newcastle United’s short-, medium- and long-term future should be considerably clearer. The day itself will see Eddie Howe either leading his team out at Wembley in the Carabao Cup final or rewatching videos of the previous day’s scheduled Premier League fixture at home to Crystal Palace. It all hinges on a two-part Carabao Cup semi-final against Arsenal, with the first leg in north London on Tuesday night. Howe has frequently reiterated his “burning desire” to end a club trophy drought stretching back to 1969 and the European Fairs Trophy. “Arsenal’s a massive game for us,” he said on Monday. “We’re under no illusions as to how big this is for our season.” Continue reading...
